items.title: Federal judge tosses state SNAP pilot limits on soda/candy purchases
items.summary: A federal judge struck down USDA-approved state pilots that limited what SNAP benefits could buy, ruling the Agriculture secretary exceeded authority under federal law. The ruling affects pilot programs in five states: Colorado, Iowa, Nebraska, Tennessee, and West Virginia.

items.title: USDA final rule raises SNAP retailer stocking standards; compliance due Nov. 4, 2026
items.summary: USDA finalized new SNAP retailer stocking standards that increase the minimum from 3 to 7 staple food varieties in each of four staple food categories. The rule is effective July 7, 2026, and SNAP retailers must implement it no later than Nov. 4, 2026.

items.title: CMS rules set Medicaid work requirement standards starting Jan. 2027; 80 hours/month
items.summary: CMS released rules detailing how Medicaid expansion enrollees must document 80 hours per month of work or other qualifying activities to keep coverage, affecting about 18.5 million people. Nebraska began enforcing requirements in May; Montana plans to start in July (with disenrollments beginning in October), and Arkansas plans a July “soft” start with no penalties until 2027.

items.title: Medicaid work rules define “half-time student” to meet 80-hour requirement
items.summary: Under CMS’ Medicaid work requirement rules, enrollees can meet the 80-hours-per-month standard through education, but the rules specify how school time counts. Students generally must be enrolled in 6 credit hours per semester to qualify as “half-time” for purposes of meeting the requirement.

items.title: WIC fruit-and-vegetable cash-value benefit set for FY 2026 (Oct 2025–Sep 2026)
items.summary: USDA issued FY 2026 WIC cash-value voucher/benefit (CVV/B) amounts: $26/month for children, $48/month for pregnant and postpartum participants, and $52/month for fully or mostly breastfeeding participants. USDA said the FY 2026 CVV/B inflation adjustment is 11.8% over the FY 2022 baseline amounts.

items.title: USDA posts WIC income eligibility guidelines for July 2026–June 2027
items.summary: USDA published WIC income eligibility guidelines effective July 1, 2026 through June 30, 2027. For the 48 contiguous states and D.C., the 185% guideline is $29,526 annually ($2,461 monthly) for a household of 1 and $61,050 annually ($5,088 monthly) for a household of 4.

items.title: Education Department lists $10M for FY 2026 Basic Needs for Postsecondary Students grants
items.summary: The U.S. Department of Education lists $10,000,000 in FY 2026 funding for its Basic Needs for Postsecondary Students Program. ED estimates 2–10 awards, with an estimated award range of $1,000,000 to $5,000,000 over a 48-month project period.

items.title: IRS publishes IRB 2026-26, including a 2026 inflation adjustment factor notice
items.summary: The IRS issued Internal Revenue Bulletin 2026-26, which includes Notice 2026-37 on the section 45 renewable electricity production credit. The bulletin says the 2026 inflation adjustment factor and reference price were published in the Federal Register at 91 FR 32511 on June 1, 2026, and lists a 2026 inflation adjustment factor of 2.0570.
